title: Moodle
description: Moodle is the world's most popular open-source learning management system (LMS) for building online courses, classrooms and e-learning.
installation: Moodle requires MySQL/MariaDB (the maintained container images target MariaDB, not PostgreSQL), so this template ships the mariadb add-on. The image is elestio/moodle, an actively-rebuilt Verified Publisher image tracking current Moodle releases (Bitnami's moodle image moved behind a paywall in 2025 and is no longer freely pullable). On first boot the container runs the Moodle installer against the database using the MOODLE_DATABASE_* env vars and provisions the admin account from MOODLE_USERNAME/MOODLE_PASSWORD/MOODLE_EMAIL; MOODLE_HOST and MOODLE_REVERSEPROXY are set so generated links resolve to the public HTTPS URL behind the ingress. After deploy, log in with the seeded admin and ROTATE: the MOODLE_PASSWORD env value, the MariaDB add-on password, and configure real SMTP under Site administration > Server > Email. Two persistent volumes are mounted: /bitnami/moodle holds the Moodle code plus config.php, and /bitnami/moodledata holds uploaded course files, caches and sessions. For production raise the app pod size (Moodle/PHP needs >=1Gi) and PHP memory_limit, and schedule the Moodle cron (admin/cli/cron.php) every minute. Optional companions: deploy the memcached add-on and point Moodle's MUC application cache at it for performance, and add an external Solr/Elasticsearch only if you enable global search (neither is required for a base install).
